howItWorks.intro: This simple interest calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is simple interest?
faq.answer: Interest calculated only on the original principal, not on accumulated interest. Formula: I = P × R × T. It is simpler but less common than compound interest.

faq.question: Where is simple interest used?
faq.answer: Auto loans, some personal loans, Treasury bonds, and short-term lending. Most savings accounts and mortgages use compound interest.

faq.question: Simple vs compound interest?
faq.answer: Simple interest grows linearly. Compound interest grows exponentially because you earn interest on interest. Over long periods, compound interest produces significantly more.

faq.question: How do I convert to monthly?
faq.answer: Divide the annual rate by 12 and the time by 12 (or multiply months by the monthly rate). Monthly simple interest = P × (R/12) × months.
